title: Executive Director
description: Lead DAT's organizational operations, financial strategy, and institutional partnerships. The ED partners with the Artistic Director to drive the company's growth at a pivotal moment in its evolution.
long_description:
As DAT completes its 20th season — twenty years of artist-led, community-rooted international theatre-making — we need an Executive Director who can hold the organization while we reach for what's next.

We've built Drama Clubs in Slovakia and Ecuador, mounted ACTion expeditions to Tanzania, Czechia, and beyond, grown an alumni network spanning continents, and developed a storytelling platform rooted in real community voices. Now we're building the infrastructure to scale thoughtfully and sustain what we've made.

This is a rare seat: direct partnership with the Artistic Director, meaningful creative proximity, and real institutional work. The ED will oversee operations, finance, development infrastructure, partnerships, and staff — and will help shape what DAT becomes over the next five years.
what_youll_do:
Oversee organizational operations, financial management, budgeting, and reporting
Manage and grow a lean staff team and network of contractors and PLX alumni
Drive institutional fundraising strategy alongside the Development team
Build and steward partnerships with funders, government bodies, universities, and community organizations
Serve as the primary liaison to the Board of Directors
Lead HR, compliance, and legal/organizational matters
Collaborate with the Artistic Director on strategic planning and season-level decisions
who_you_are:
A builder — you've run organizations or departments before and you know what good systems feel like
Comfortable in financial complexity — budgets, reporting, and sustainability planning
A collaborative partner — you bring out the best in creative colleagues without needing to be the creative one
Committed to DAT's values: cross-cultural exchange, community rootedness, artistic rigor
Low-ego, high-ownership, ready to roll up your sleeves
requirements:
5+ years of nonprofit leadership or equivalent experience
Financial management experience — budgets, cash flow, audit-readiness
Strong background in fundraising, grant management, or development infrastructure
Proven experience managing staff or teams
US-based or willing to work US hours
Ability to travel internationally at least once per year
perks:
Compensation range shared with qualified applicants
Open to full-time, fractional, or phased arrangements depending on fit and funding
Remote-first; in-person presence expected for key organizational moments
Creative proximity to a working international theatre company
Benefits and travel expectations discussed during the offer process
timeline:
Applications Open :: Rolling
Introductory Calls :: Ongoing
Target Start :: Fall 2026 or Winter 2027
faq:
Is the salary posted? :: We share the range directly with qualified applicants. Write to jobs@dramaticadventure.com to learn more.
Does DAT have a Board? :: Yes. The ED reports to the Board and works closely with the Artistic Director.
Is this truly remote? :: Core team is distributed. In-person presence is expected for key organizational moments and field work. Field travel may be available when aligned with program needs and funding; travel is discussed before engagement and is not a substitute for agreed compensation.

title: Development & Partnerships Lead
description: Build and steward DAT's funding base and institutional relationships. Lead grant strategy, major-donor cultivation, and the partnerships that sustain DAT's international work.
compensation: Salary or contract rate commensurate with experience
long_description:
DAT has a loyal donor base, a compelling story, and a track record of producing work across three continents. What we need is someone who can build the infrastructure around all that — a Development & Partnerships Lead who treats fundraising as a craft, not a chore.

This role is open to a full-time hire or a fractional/contract arrangement for the right person. You'll drive our grant calendar, deepen individual-donor relationships, and build the institutional connections that make international theatre possible.

Ecuador-based candidates, especially those based in Quito, are strongly encouraged to express interest. Bilingual Spanish/English candidates are especially welcome. This role may connect directly to DAT's Ecuador hub-building work, including ACTion, Drama Club, and local sponsor cultivation.
what_youll_do:
Lead DAT's annual grant calendar — research, write, track, report
Cultivate and steward a portfolio of individual major donors
Identify and pursue new institutional partnerships (universities, foundations, government arts agencies, corporate sponsors)
Manage our development database and donor communications cadence
Draft annual-fund campaigns, year-end appeals, and campaign-specific donor materials
Represent DAT at key partnership meetings and convenings
Collaborate with Comms on donor-facing storytelling
who_you_are:
An experienced development professional with a strong writing portfolio
Comfortable asking for money and comfortable cultivating the relationship before the ask
Organized, deadline-driven, and genuinely curious about DAT's work
Able to work independently and communicate proactively
requirements:
3+ years of nonprofit development experience
Demonstrated grant-writing success
Strong relationship-management instincts
Proficient with a CRM (Salesforce NPSP or similar)
US-based, remote-capable
perks:
Compensation commensurate with experience (salary or contract)
Meaningful mission with direct creative proximity
Remote-first
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
Direct mentorship from DAT's Artistic Director and senior leadership
Priority consideration for expanded roles as DAT grows
Field travel may be available when aligned with program needs and funding; travel is discussed before engagement and is not a substitute for agreed compensation

Introductory Calls :: Ongoing
Target Start :: Fall 2026

title: Managing Producer / Program Operations Lead
description: Keep DAT's multi-continent program engine running. The Managing Producer owns logistics, timelines, budgets, and coordination across productions, residencies, and program cycles.
compensation: Salary or contract rate commensurate with experience
long_description:
DAT's work runs across multiple countries, programs, and time zones simultaneously. Someone has to hold the map.

The Managing Producer is the operational core of the company: tracking timelines, managing program budgets, coordinating artists and staff, and making sure the productions, residencies, ACTion expeditions, and Drama Club seasons all stay on track and on budget. This is not a desk job. It's a craft role for someone who loves the systems that make creative work possible.
what_youll_do:
Manage production and program timelines across all active DAT projects
Oversee logistics for ACTion expeditions — travel, housing, visas, on-the-ground coordination
Build and track program budgets in partnership with the ED
Coordinate artists, staff, contractors, and local partners across productions
Manage the PLX program calendar and mentorship schedules
Keep project documentation current — reports, contracts, tracking sheets
Identify and problem-solve operational bottlenecks before they become crises
who_you_are:
A producer by instinct — you see the whole board and the next three moves
Comfortable with international logistics and the chaos that comes with them
Detail-oriented without losing the big picture
Calm under pressure, generous in a crunch
requirements:
3+ years producing theatre, events, or international programs
Strong project-management skills (Asana, Notion, or similar)
Budget management experience
Ability to travel internationally 1–2 times per year
Remote-capable and proactive communicator
perks:
Compensation commensurate with experience
Creative proximity to a working international theatre company
Remote-first
Opportunity to travel to a DAT project in the field (subject to production calendar and funding)
timeline:
Expressions of Interest :: Open now — write to jobs@dramaticadventure.com
Target Start :: Fall 2026

The Communications & Story Lead will shape the editorial and content infrastructure that lets DAT's work speak — across social media, the alumni storytelling platform, donor communications, press, and digital campaigns. title: Local Project Producer
description: Lead local production logistics for a specific DAT project — from an ACTion expedition to a Drama Club season or community residency. Project-based role tied to a specific geography and timeline.
long_description:
DAT's next wave of projects — PASSAGE Slovakia 2026, Drama Club expansions, new community residencies in Ecuador and beyond — each needs a producer on the ground who knows the territory.

The Local Project Producer is a project-based contract role: you come in for a specific project, own the local logistics, and leave with a strong credit and a standing invitation to come back. We're building a bench of project producers across our active hubs as DAT builds toward Season 21 and beyond.

Ecuador-based and Quito-based candidates are especially encouraged to express interest. Bilingual Spanish/English is strongly preferred for Ecuador engagements. This role helps make Ecuador not just a program site, but a year-round DAT hub.
what_youll_do:
Own logistics for a specific DAT project in your city or region
Coordinate with local partners, venues, housing, and transport
Manage the local project budget and timeline
Serve as primary liaison between DAT HQ and on-the-ground reality
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs
Document the project for DAT's records and storytelling channels
who_you_are:
Based in or deeply familiar with the project hub (Slovakia, Ecuador/Quito, NYC, etc.)
A practical, resourceful producer — you've managed logistics under pressure
Warm, bridge-building energy
Familiar with local arts, cultural, or community organizations
For Ecuador engagements: bilingual Spanish/English strongly preferred; Quito-based candidates especially welcome

2+ years of producing or event/project management experience
Based in or able to relocate to the project hub for the duration
References from prior production or community work

DAT alumni network access

Secure and confirm venues, housing, and transport for all stops on the route

Manage the local project budget and logistics timeline
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs in the field

who_you_are:
Based in Slovakia or with deep working knowledge of the country
A practical, resourceful producer — you’ve managed logistics under pressure
Fluent or highly functional in Slovak (English also required)
Connected to local arts, cultural, or community organizations
Available to commit to the full pre-production and in-field window

2+ years of producing, event management, or project coordination experience
Strong working knowledge of at least two route regions: Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, or Slovenský Raj
References from prior production or community work
Fluency or working proficiency in Slovak
perks:

First right of refusal on future DAT projects in Slovakia or Central Europe

faq:
What’s the difference between this roster and PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026? :: PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 is a specific upcoming project with an artist participation fee model — you pay to participate. The Traveling Ensemble Artist Roster is for future paid ensemble projects where DAT places artists on contract. Apply to both if you’re interested in either.
What does ‘devised’ mean exactly? :: We build the piece together from scratch — no pre-existing script. Come with stories, movement, and questions.
How often do ensemble projects happen? :: Roughly one to two international ensemble projects per season. DAT is expanding its project calendar as it builds toward Season 21.

title: PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 — Traveling Artist
description: Travel with a small international ensemble across Slovakia — Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, and Slovenský Raj — July 12–August 2, 2026. Create original work shaped by place, encounter, and collective imagination. An artist participation fee applies.
compensation: Artist participation fee applies; amount shared with accepted applicants
long_description:
PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 is an immersive ensemble experience — part artistic journey, part cultural exchange, part creative development process, part collective making. A small international ensemble travels together through Slovakia, making original work shaped by place, encounter, conversation, and imagination.

The journey runs July 12–August 2, 2026, moving through Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, and Slovenský Raj. The project culminates in a final shared performance on August 1 in Košice.

PASSAGE is not a paid artist gig. It operates on an artist participation fee model — similar to an international residency or intensive, but in motion and in community. You contribute to the cost of the ensemble experience; what you make and carry back is yours. DAT organizes the ensemble, the logistics, the community partnerships, and the creative framework. You bring your craft, your curiosity, and your willingness to be changed by what you find.
what_youll_do:
Travel with a small international ensemble through four regions of Slovakia
Make original devised work shaped by place, community encounters, and the journey itself
Participate in workshops, cultural exchanges, and reflection sessions throughout
Contribute to the final shared performance on August 1 in Košice
Document your process through writing, image, or your own artistic lens
Engage genuinely with local artists, youth, and community members along the route
who_you_are:
A practicing artist — performer, deviser, writer, maker — with ensemble experience
Curious and adaptable across cultural and linguistic contexts
A listener first — genuinely interested in other people’s stories
Ready to make work in motion, without a fixed script or known outcome
Comfortable with shared living and ensemble travel
requirements:
18 years or older
Valid passport with 6+ months remaining beyond August 2026
Ability to commit to the full in-field window: July 12 – August 2, 2026
Artist participation fee (amount shared with accepted applicants; payment plan available)
Experience in devised, physical, or community-engaged performance preferred
perks:
22 days of ensemble travel through an extraordinary cultural landscape
Original work made with and for real communities
Portfolio documentation of the journey and final performance
DAT alumni artist network
Creative credit on all PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 documentation
timeline:
Applications Open :: Now — rolling review
Application Deadline :: June 15, 2026 (or until ensemble is full)
Ensemble Decisions :: By late June 2026
Pre-Departure Orientation :: Online, early July 2026
In-Field :: July 12 – August 2, 2026
Final Performance :: August 1, 2026, Košice
faq:
Is this a paid artist job? :: No. PASSAGE operates on an artist participation fee model — you contribute to the cost of the ensemble experience. Think of it like an international residency or intensive, but in motion. The fee amount is shared with accepted applicants.
What’s included in the participation fee? :: In-country logistics, shared accommodation, ground transport, and production costs for the final performance. International travel to/from Slovakia is not included.
Do I need to speak Slovak? :: No. The ensemble works in English with local translation support. Curiosity and warmth carry further than language here.
What kind of work will we make? :: Devised, site-responsive, ensemble work — built from scratch on the road. No pre-existing script. Come with stories, questions, and a practice that can travel.
Is housing communal? :: Yes. Ensemble housing is shared throughout the journey. If that’s a constraint, reach out and we’ll talk through the options.

title: Dramaturg / Researcher
description: Dramaturgs and researchers for DAT's active productions and residencies. Deep knowledge of cultural context, world theatre, or community-based practice especially valued.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's productions are research-rich — built from history, community stories, cultural context, and textual adaptation. We work with dramaturgs who can hold the intellectual and ethical weight of that research, translate it for an ensemble, and stay inside the creative process as the work evolves.

If you have deep knowledge of Central Europe, Latin America, East Africa, or another DAT hub region, we especially want to hear from you.
what_youll_do:
Conduct contextual research for a specific production or residency
Build and maintain a dramaturgical archive
Present research to the ensemble in accessible, actionable form
Advise on cultural accuracy, representation, and ethical storytelling
Attend rehearsals or workshops when relevant
who_you_are:
A dramaturg or researcher with experience in community-engaged or world-theatre contexts
Intellectually rigorous and creatively flexible
Committed to ethical representation of community stories
requirements:
18 years or older
Dramaturgical or research portfolio
Language skills or deep cultural knowledge of DAT's hub regions is a significant plus
perks:
Dramaturgical fee when placed
Credits on production materials
DAT alumni artist network
timeline:
Rolling :: Submit anytime
Reviewed :: Quarterly

title: Teaching Artist
description: Teaching artists for DAT's Drama Clubs, school residencies, and community storytelling programs. We place teaching artists locally and internationally — across NYC, Slovakia, Ecuador, and beyond.
commitment: Varies by project (semester, residency, or workshop-based)
compensation: Teaching artist fee; rate varies by program
long_description:
DAT's teaching artist practice is the heartbeat of the organization — rooted in Drama Club seasons, school residencies, community storytelling workshops, and the training of local teaching artists in each hub we work in.

We place teaching artists who are genuine artists first, who bring their practice into rooms with young people and community members, and who understand the difference between teaching skills and opening possibility. If you are a teaching artist — or an artist who wants to develop a teaching practice — we want to know about you.
what_youll_do:
Lead Drama Club sessions, school residencies, or community workshops
Design curriculum in collaboration with DAT's education team
Develop youth and community voices through storytelling, devising, and performance
Document your teaching practice for DAT's educational archive
Participate in DAT's teaching artist training and professional development
Travel to international hubs if relevant and available
who_you_are:
An artist first — a practitioner with a real creative practice
Warm, patient, and energized by working with young people and communities
Comfortable in both structured and open-ended educational settings
Interested in developing and refining your teaching artist pedagogy
requirements:
18 years or older
Experience as a teaching artist or artist-educator
Work samples and references required
Background check required for youth-serving programs
Passport and willingness to travel for international placements
perks:
Teaching artist fee (rate by program)
International placement opportunities
Formal pedagogy training through DAT
DAT alumni teaching artist network
Letter of recommendation available
timeline:
Rolling :: Submit anytime
Placement :: When the right program opening matches your profile
faq:
Do I need curriculum-writing experience? :: Helpful but not required. We train you on DAT's curriculum frameworks.
Are international placements paid? :: Yes. Travel and housing are included for international residency placements.

title: Local Artist / Cultural Collaborator
description: Local artists, cultural practitioners, and community members who partner with DAT's visiting ensembles. Based in one of DAT's hub cities — Slovakia, Ecuador, Tanzania, or beyond — and open to making work across cultures.
compensation: Collaboration fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work only works because of local artists and cultural collaborators. When we arrive in Bratislava, Quito, or Bagamoyo, we come to listen — and local artists are the people we listen to first and most.

This call is for artists, cultural practitioners, community leaders, and creative people who are based in a DAT hub region and open to collaborating with an international ensemble. The relationship is reciprocal and honest.
what_youll_do:
Introduce DAT's visiting ensemble to your community, practice, and place
Co-create material for an original devised piece
Lead or co-lead a community workshop from your own practice
Serve as a cultural guide and creative collaborator throughout the residency
Perform in or co-present the final work if relevant and desired
who_you_are:
A local artist, cultural practitioner, community leader, or creative person based in a DAT hub region
Genuinely interested in cross-cultural creative exchange
Comfortable in collaborative and ensemble settings
requirements:
16 years or older
Based in or deeply connected to a DAT hub region
Ability to communicate in English (with support) or in the local language with a translator
perks:
Collaboration fee when engaged
Documentation and portfolio materials
Cross-cultural artistic exchange
DAT alumni international network
timeline:
Rolling :: Register your interest anytime
Matched :: When the right project in your region opens
faq: Which regions does DAT currently work in? :: Slovakia/Czechia, Ecuador, Tanzania, and NYC are our primary active hubs. We're scouting Australia and other regions.

description: Designers for DAT's productions — set, costume, lighting, sound, or projection. We work in non-traditional spaces and value designers who can make meaningful work with limited resources.
compensation: Design fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT makes theatre in fields, courtyards, community centers, and found spaces around the world. Our designers are creative problem-solvers who work with the space, not against it — and who find beauty in constraints. We're building a roster of designers across disciplines for our upcoming productions and residencies.
what_youll_do:

Get matched to design opportunities on upcoming productions
Collaborate with the director and ensemble on design concept
Execute design within production constraints (budget, space, travel)

Resourceful and collaborative
Comfortable with ambiguity and design constraints

Experience designing for non-traditional spaces preferred

long_description: DAT's stage managers are the production's center of gravity — holding the process together across devising, community workshops, technical rehearsals, and performance. We work in unusual spaces, across cultures, and often without a full technical infrastructure. If you bring rigor to the unexpected, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do:

Coordinate with designers, technical staff, and artists
Call the show, manage the deck, manage the process

Precise, calm, and relationship-forward

We work with musicians who can compose, improvise, teach, and perform in the same breath, and with sound artists who understand the texture of a room, a community, a culture. We're building a roster of musicians, composers, and sound artists for our upcoming productions and residencies.

title: Documentation Artist
description: Photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers for DAT's productions and residencies. We want documentation that captures the truth of the work — not just the performance.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work happens in fields, studios, community centers, and living rooms around the world — and most of it is never seen by anyone outside the room. The Documentation Artist changes that.

We're building a roster of photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers who want to capture the real texture of the work — the listening, the rehearsal, the moment before the show, the meal afterward. If you can make a frame that tells the truth, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do:
Document productions, workshops, and community sessions in the field
Build a project archive — photo, video, audio
Collaborate with the Comms team on content production
Develop your own documentary project around the work
Contribute to DAT's permanent institutional archive

Rolling :: Submit anytime

title: Board Committee Member
description: Serve on a DAT Board committee — Finance, Governance, Development, or Artistic Advisory. Governance-level engagement without full Board commitment. For people who believe in DAT’s mission and bring relevant expertise.
long_description:
DAT’s Board committees are where mission meets expertise. We’re building committees in Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory — each a focused group of people who bring real knowledge and care to DAT’s institutional work.

Committee members are not full Board members. They advise, review, and contribute on specific questions — without the full fiduciary commitment of Board service. This is a meaningful, low-overhead way to support an organization doing unusual work.
what_youll_do:
Attend committee meetings (typically 4–6 per year, virtual)
Review materials relevant to your committee’s focus (finance reports, governance docs, grant proposals, etc.)
Provide expert input, ask hard questions, and help DAT think clearly
Connect DAT staff to relevant contacts in your network where appropriate
Support the full Board’s decision-making with committee recommendations
who_you_are:
A professional with expertise in finance, law, governance, development, or the arts
Genuinely interested in DAT’s mission and the sector it works in
Willing to engage seriously with the organization’s real challenges
No formal governance or Board experience required — orientation provided
requirements:
18 years or older
Relevant professional or lived expertise
Ability to attend virtual committee meetings (approximately monthly)
Comfort with confidential organizational information
perks:
Meaningful governance engagement without full Board commitment
DAT community access and invitations to programs, salons, and artistic convenings
Direct engagement with DAT's Artistic Director and Board leadership
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
Letter of reference available
timeline: Rolling :: Join a committee anytime — next meeting scheduled upon onboarding
faq:
What committees are active? :: Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory. Tell us your background and we’ll suggest the right fit.
Does this lead to a full Board seat? :: It can, but it doesn’t have to. Many committee members stay as committee members indefinitely.

title: Host Committee Member
description: Lend your name, network, and enthusiasm to a DAT event, campaign, or fundraiser. Help us reach people we wouldn’t otherwise reach — by making a personal ask, sharing the event, or simply showing up.
long_description:
DAT’s host committees are loose, event-specific networks of people who believe in the work and are willing to say so publicly. For each major event or campaign, we invite a small group to lend their names, share the event with their networks, and make a personal ask to a few people they think would care.

This is not a standing commitment. Each host committee is formed for a specific purpose and dissolves when the event or campaign ends. You opt in each time.
what_youll_do:
Lend your name to a specific DAT event, fundraiser, or campaign
Share the event with people in your network via email or personal message
Make a personal ask to two or three people you think would genuinely respond
Attend the event or campaign launch where possible
who_you_are:
Someone who believes in DAT’s work and is willing to say so
Connected to people who might attend, donate, or get involved
Comfortable making a personal (not mass) ask on behalf of something you believe in
requirements:
No formal requirements — this is about your enthusiasm and your network
Willingness to make a personal ask to at least two or three people
perks:
Invitations to all DAT events you help host
DAT community access and social inclusion at events
Recognition in event materials (optional)
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network
timeline: Rolling :: We invite host committee members on a per-event basis — let us know you’re interested and we’ll reach out when the next one comes together
faq:
Is this a recurring commitment? :: No. Each host committee is event-specific. You opt in each time — no ongoing obligation.
Do I need to make a donation? :: Not required, but appreciated. The most valuable thing is your personal outreach to people who wouldn’t otherwise hear about DAT.

title: Drama Club Teaching Artist Lead
description: Lead DAT’s teaching artist work in an active Drama Club hub — Slovakia or Ecuador. Facilitate original storytelling sessions with young people, mentor local teaching artists, and help a community realize that their stories matter. Project-based contract.
compensation: Project-based contract fee; rate shared with qualified applicants
long_description:
DAT's Drama Clubs exist because young people deserve space to tell their own stories — and because communities are stronger when those stories are heard.

The Drama Club Teaching Artist Lead facilitates DAT's storytelling and devising process with youth participants in an active hub (currently Slovakia and Ecuador). You'll bring artistic skill, cultural intelligence, and a genuine belief in young people's stories.

Ecuador-based and Quito-based Teaching Artists are especially encouraged. Spanish proficiency is strongly preferred for Ecuador hubs. This role is part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team for Ecuador-based engagements — you'd be helping build the year-round teaching infrastructure behind DAT's Drama Club programs.
what_youll_do:
Facilitate original storytelling and devising sessions with youth participants
Adapt DAT’s methodology to the local context, culture, and age group
Mentor and co-facilitate with local teaching artists and community co-leads
Guide participants from story collection through to performance or sharing
Document sessions and report on participant development to DAT program staff
Coordinate with DAT on session design, arc, and community relationships
who_you_are:
An experienced teaching artist with a genuine practice in devising or storytelling
Patient, adaptable, and skilled at building trust with young people
Comfortable working across language and culture — ideally with prior cross-cultural experience
Motivated by community impact, not just performance credits
Willing to listen more than you speak, at least at first
requirements:
2+ years of teaching artist or youth theatre facilitation experience
Experience with devised or community-engaged practice
Ability to commit to the full Drama Club session arc
References from prior teaching artist or community work
For Slovakia hubs: comfort with Central European context; for Ecuador: Spanish proficiency strongly preferred
perks:
Project-based contract fee (rate discussed with qualified applicants)
Housing support for non-local hires where applicable
Deep creative and community immersion
Professional title and production credits
DAT alumni and artist network access
Priority consideration for future Drama Club and PLX program roles
For Ecuador-based artists: part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team
timeline:
Inquire Now :: Season 21 Drama Clubs planned for 2026–2027; engagement timing to be confirmed
Target Engagement :: Fall 2026 or Spring 2027 depending on hub
faq:
Which hubs are active? :: Slovakia and Ecuador are our current active Drama Club hubs. Other hubs may open as DAT builds toward Season 21.
Do I need to speak Slovak or Spanish? :: Slovak is helpful but not required for Slovakia hubs — we work with local co-facilitators and translators. Spanish proficiency is strongly preferred for Ecuador.

title: Ecuador-Based Development & Partnerships Lead
description: Build DAT's funding base and institutional relationships in Ecuador and the Andean region. Cultivate local sponsors, embassies, universities, and donors; support ACTion, Drama Club, and hub-building campaigns. Bilingual Spanish/English strongly preferred. Part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
commitment: Part-time or fractional (10–25 hrs/wk depending on scope)
compensation: Part-time contract, stipend, fellowship, or low-retainer arrangement; amount to be finalized based on scope, funding, and candidate location. DAT is open to phased arrangements that can grow with revenue.
long_description:
DAT's Ecuador work — ACTion expeditions, Drama Clubs, Teaching Artist Residencies — needs local institutional roots to become sustainable. This role helps build those roots.

The Ecuador-Based Development & Partnerships Lead will research and cultivate local donors, sponsors, foundations, universities, embassies, and cultural institutes in Ecuador and the Andean region. You'll be part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team — helping make Quito not just a program site, but a year-round organizational hub.

DAT is rebuilding its paid capacity. This role is best understood as a part-time contract, stipend, fellowship, or low-retainer arrangement to start, with real room to grow as revenue and funding develop. We are committed to being honest about scope, expectations, and what DAT can offer in return — and to building an arrangement that works for the right candidate.

This role is best for someone who values real responsibility, mentorship, international arts access, portfolio-building work, and the chance to help build a lasting creative hub.
what_youll_do:
Research Ecuadorian and regional foundations, corporate sponsors, embassies, and cultural institutes with potential interest in DAT's work
Build and steward relationships with Quito-based donors, sponsors, and institutional partners
Support DAT fundraising campaigns connected to Ecuador programs (ACTion, Drama Clubs, sponsor-a-fellow, etc.)
Coordinate or support small Quito-based donor events and salon gatherings
Draft Spanish-language sponsor and partnership materials in coordination with DAT's Comms team
Represent DAT at relevant Quito arts, education, and cultural convenings
Maintain records of Ecuador-based prospects and relationships in DAT's CRM
who_you_are:
Based in Quito or Ecuador (strongly preferred) — you know the local arts, education, nonprofit, or business landscape
Bilingual Spanish/English — you can write and speak comfortably in both languages
A relationship-builder with patience for the long game of institutional development
Organized, entrepreneurial, and comfortable working with limited infrastructure at a growth-stage organization
Genuinely committed to DAT's mission and to Ecuador's role as a real hub — not just a program destination
requirements:
Experience in development, fundraising, partnerships, nonprofit administration, or related field
Bilingual Spanish/English strongly preferred
Based in Ecuador strongly preferred; Quito-based strongly encouraged
Ability to work independently and communicate proactively with DAT HQ (US hours overlap helpful)
References from prior professional or community work
perks:
Part-time contract, stipend, fellowship, or low-retainer arrangement — phased growth possible as funding develops
Real ownership of DAT's Ecuador development infrastructure — not an assistant role
Professional title and credits: Ecuador-Based Development & Partnerships Lead, DAT Ecuador Founding Hub Team
Direct mentorship from DAT's Artistic Director, Development team, and senior advisors
Training in arts administration, nonprofit fundraising, international producing, and cross-cultural collaboration
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network across three continents
Priority consideration for future expanded paid roles as DAT's Ecuador hub grows
Invitations to DAT donor events, salons, and artistic convenings (local and international)
Letter of recommendation after successful engagement
Field travel may be available when aligned with program needs and funding; travel is discussed before engagement and is not a substitute for agreed compensation
timeline:
Expressions of Interest :: Open now — write to jobs@dramaticadventure.com
Target Start :: Fall 2026 or earlier depending on fit
faq:
Does this role require in-person presence in Quito? :: Quito-based or Ecuador-based is strongly preferred. Some responsibilities — local events, partner meetings — are most effective in person. Remote arrangements may be possible for the right candidate.
Is the compensation posted? :: Compensation will be discussed directly with qualified candidates. DAT is open to part-time contract, stipend, fellowship, or phased retainer arrangements that can grow with revenue.
What does 'Founding Hub Team' mean? :: DAT is building year-round organizational infrastructure in Ecuador — not just running programs there. Early team members in Ecuador are foundational to that project and will shape what it becomes.

title: Ecuador Local Producer / Ground Coordinator
description: Project-based producing and logistics coordination for DAT programs in Ecuador — ACTion expeditions, Drama Club seasons, Teaching Artist Residencies, and field work across the country. Paid when active. Bilingual Spanish/English required. Part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
commitment: Project-based (varies by program; typically 1–3 months per engagement)
compensation: Paid when placed; project contract fee varies by program and funding. In-field housing, meals, or in-kind support may be included depending on the project; all logistics expectations are discussed before engagement.
long_description:
When DAT is active in Ecuador — an ACTion expedition, a Drama Club season, a Teaching Artist Residency, a community performance — we need a producer on the ground who knows the territory.

The Ecuador Local Producer / Ground Coordinator is a project-based role: you come in for a specific engagement, own the local logistics, and leave with a strong credit and a standing invitation to come back. We're building a reliable bench of Ecuador-based producers as DAT grows its year-round Ecuador footprint.

Paid when active; contract fee varies by project and funding. In-field housing, meals, or other in-kind support may be included depending on the program. Travel, out-of-pocket expectations, and logistics support are discussed clearly before engagement begins.

This role is part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
what_youll_do:
Own local logistics for a specific DAT program in Ecuador — housing, venues, transport, hospitality, permits
Coordinate with local community partners, schools, artists, and venues
Manage the local project budget and logistics timeline in partnership with DAT's Managing Producer
Serve as primary liaison between DAT HQ and on-the-ground reality during the program
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs
Document the program for DAT's records and storytelling channels
Build and maintain local vendor and partner relationships between engagements
who_you_are:
Based in Ecuador — you know Quito well and have working knowledge of DAT's program regions
Bilingual Spanish/English — essential for this role
A practical, resourceful producer — you've managed logistics under pressure and solve problems before they become crises
Warm and bridge-building — you connect DAT artists with communities with care and cultural intelligence
Familiar with Ecuador's arts, education, or community organization landscape
requirements:

title: Bilingual Communications & Story Coordinator — Ecuador
description: Collect and shape stories from DAT's Ecuador programs — Drama Club youth, Teaching Artists, ACTion participants, local collaborators. Support bilingual Spanish/English content creation, photo/video coordination, local press, and campaign materials. Stipend or fellowship arrangement. Part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
compensation: Stipend, fellowship, or project contract; amount to be finalized based on scope and funding. DAT is open to phased arrangements.
long_description:
DAT's Ecuador programs generate extraordinary material — Drama Club youth discovering their voices, communities transformed by ACTion expeditions, Teaching Artists building cross-cultural bridges. But capturing those stories in Spanish and in English, for local audiences and for DAT's global community, requires someone who can live inside both languages and both worlds.

The Bilingual Communications & Story Coordinator — Ecuador supports Spanish/English story collection, Drama Club documentation, local artist interviews, photo and video coordination, campaign materials, and outreach to Ecuadorian press and storytelling platforms.

This role may begin as a stipend, fellowship, or project contract depending on scope and funding. We are committed to being honest about expectations and to making the learning and professional-development value real.

This role is part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
what_youll_do:
Conduct story interviews with Drama Club youth, Teaching Artists, local collaborators, and ACTion participants in Spanish and English
Draft and edit bilingual content — stories, social posts, donor updates, program descriptions — for DAT's platforms
Coordinate photo and video documentation during Ecuador programs
Prepare Spanish-language versions of campaign materials, program descriptions, and community communications
Support outreach to Ecuadorian local press, arts publications, and storytelling platforms
Work closely with DAT's Communications & Story Lead (US-based) to align Ecuador content with DAT's editorial voice
who_you_are:
Fluent in both Spanish and English — you write naturally and with voice in both languages
A storyteller by instinct — you know how to find the moment inside a conversation
Based in Ecuador or with deep Ecuador roots
Comfortable with basic photo or video documentation
Interested in arts, community, theatre, or youth development
requirements:
Bilingual Spanish/English — strong written proficiency in both languages required
Writing portfolio or samples in at least one language
Based in Ecuador strongly preferred
Experience in journalism, communications, storytelling, arts documentation, or related field helpful
perks:
Stipend, fellowship, or project contract — amount confirmed based on scope and funding
Published bylines and content credits in Spanish and English across DAT's platforms
Portfolio of published work samples
Direct mentorship from DAT's Communications & Story Lead and Artistic Director
Training in documentary storytelling, arts communications, and cross-cultural content strategy
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
In-program field access — when programs are active in Ecuador, this role is in the room
Invitations to DAT convenings, donor events, and field briefings
Letter of recommendation after successful engagement
